type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Ethylene Glycol is used at 5 Wing goose Bay to de-ice aircraft. During 2004 a larger quantity of transient military aircraft passed through 5 Wind due to exit of permanent allied foreces from 5 Wing Goose Bay.  this resulted in larger cargo planes requiri

type.fr: Commentaire sur l'élimination

comments.comment: The primary source of lead at 5 Wing Goose Bay is lead based paint from building demolitions.  teh emount has decreased from previous year due to a moratorium on any demolition projects at the Wing.  Some buildings may be removed if a health and safety ha

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: During 2004 there was no active range on the Wing resulting in zero emissions to the air from ammunition.  However, in January 2005, a new range was developed at the Wing and future reports may include air emissions from its use.

comments.comment: No glycol is collected after application to the aircraft.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Aircraft de-icing in Goose Bay is primarly completed for civilian operations by a private contractor for the Airport Corporation. However, the airport exists on DND property.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
Land releases attributable to the Wing rifle range. The range use was limited in 2003, resulting in less ammunition fired.

Range is scheduled for closure and anticipated releases will be nil for the next several years.

comments.comment: We did not report last year.  Large values of lead result of building deconstruction project of a 6-story building approximately 50 years old.
